page: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7542 processors running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing 6.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a 2048 GB SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads perform significantly better. Passmark CPU scores, extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests all rank in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench ray tracing also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. Redis SET operations show high efficiency, reaching 72.18 million operations per second.

The shared CPU allocation and lower clock speed present a tradeoff, resulting in slower single-threaded execution and poor text generation speeds for medium-sized LLM models. However, the high core density and substantial memory capacity offer qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads that do not require dedicated CPU pinning. This server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and parallel data processing tasks.

It includes a bundled 2048 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address. While single-core performance is limited, ranking in the bottom 10% in stress-ng benchmarks, the server excels in multi-threaded operations. Passmark CPU, encryption, and physics tests place in the top 25%, and Redis SET operations reach 72.18 million operations per second. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for single-threaded tasks but offers resource density for parallel workloads. This configuration is suitable for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving.

tagline: A high-core-density virtual server featuring 384 GB RAM and local SSD storage, optimized for parallelized multi-threaded workloads.

Strong multi-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for Passmark CPU tests
